tables.name: audit_log_entries
tables.comment: Auth: Audit trail for user actions.

owner.name: owner
owner.comment: Field is deprecated, use owner_id instead

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX buckets_analytics_unique_name_idx ON storage.buckets_analytics USING btree (name) WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL)

tables.name: flow_state
tables.comment: Stores metadata for all OAuth/SSO login flows

email.name: email
email.comment: Auth: Email is a generated column that references the optional email property in the identity_data

tables.name: identities
tables.comment: Auth: Stores identities associated to a user.

tables.name: instances
tables.comment: Auth: Manages users across multiple sites.

indexes.expression: CREATE INDEX messages_inserted_at_topic_index ON ONLY realtime.messages USING btree (inserted_at DESC, topic) WHERE ((extension = 'broadcast'::text) AND (private IS TRUE))

tables.name: mfa_amr_claims
tables.comment: auth: stores authenticator method reference claims for multi factor authentication

tables.name: mfa_challenges
tables.comment: auth: stores metadata about challenge requests made

last_webauthn_challenge_data.name: last_webauthn_challenge_data
last_webauthn_challenge_data.comment: Stores the latest WebAuthn challenge data including attestation/assertion for customer verification

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX mfa_factors_user_friendly_name_unique ON auth.mfa_factors USING btree (friendly_name, user_id) WHERE (TRIM(BOTH FROM friendly_name) <> ''::text)
CREATE INDEX oauth_auth_pending_exp_idx ON auth.oauth_authorizations USING btree (expires_at) WHERE (status = 'pending'::auth.oauth_authorization_status)

tables.name: oauth_client_states
tables.comment: Stores OAuth states for third-party provider authentication flows where Supabase acts as the OAuth client.

indexes.expression: CREATE INDEX oauth_consents_active_client_idx ON auth.oauth_consents USING btree (client_id) WHERE (revoked_at IS NULL)
CREATE INDEX oauth_consents_active_user_client_idx ON auth.oauth_consents USING btree (user_id, client_id) WHERE (revoked_at IS NULL)

owner.name: owner
owner.comment: Field is deprecated, use owner_id instead

indexes.expression: CREATE INDEX idx_objects_bucket_id_name_lower ON storage.objects USING btree (bucket_id, lower(name) COLLATE "C")

tables.name: refresh_tokens
tables.comment: Auth: Store of tokens used to refresh JWT tokens once they expire.

tables.name: saml_providers
tables.comment: Auth: Manages SAML Identity Provider connections.

tables.name: saml_relay_states
tables.comment: Auth: Contains SAML Relay State information for each Service Provider initiated login.

tables.name: schema_migrations
tables.comment: Auth: Manages updates to the auth system.

tables.name: secrets

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX secrets_name_idx ON vault.secrets USING btree (name) WHERE (name IS NOT NULL)

tables.comment: Table with encrypted `secret` column for storing sensitive information on disk.

not_after.name: not_after
not_after.comment: Auth: Not after is a nullable column that contains a timestamp after which the session should be regarded as expired.

refresh_token_hmac_key.name: refresh_token_hmac_key
refresh_token_hmac_key.comment: Holds a HMAC-SHA256 key used to sign refresh tokens for this session.

refresh_token_counter.name: refresh_token_counter
refresh_token_counter.comment: Holds the ID (counter) of the last issued refresh token.

tables.name: sessions
tables.comment: Auth: Stores session data associated to a user.

tables.name: sso_domains

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX sso_domains_domain_idx ON auth.sso_domains USING btree (lower(domain))

tables.comment: Auth: Manages SSO email address domain mapping to an SSO Identity Provider.

resource_id.name: resource_id
resource_id.comment: Auth: Uniquely identifies a SSO provider according to a user-chosen resource ID (case insensitive), useful in infrastructure as code.

tables.name: sso_providers

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX sso_providers_resource_id_idx ON auth.sso_providers USING btree (lower(resource_id))

tables.comment: Auth: Manages SSO identity provider information; see saml_providers for SAML.

is_sso_user.name: is_sso_user
is_sso_user.comment: Auth: Set this column to true when the account comes from SSO. These accounts can have duplicate emails.

tables.name: users

indexes.expression: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX confirmation_token_idx ON auth.users USING btree (confirmation_token) WHERE ((confirmation_token)::text !~ '^[0-9 ]*$'::text)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX email_change_token_current_idx ON auth.users USING btree (email_change_token_current) WHERE ((email_change_token_current)::text !~ '^[0-9 ]*$'::text)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX email_change_token_new_idx ON auth.users USING btree (email_change_token_new) WHERE ((email_change_token_new)::text !~ '^[0-9 ]*$'::text)
CREATE INDEX idx_users_name ON auth.users USING btree (((raw_user_meta_data ->> 'name'::text))) WHERE ((raw_user_meta_data ->> 'name'::text) IS NOT NULL)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX reauthentication_token_idx ON auth.users USING btree (reauthentication_token) WHERE ((reauthentication_token)::text !~ '^[0-9 ]*$'::text)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX recovery_token_idx ON auth.users USING btree (recovery_token) WHERE ((recovery_token)::text !~ '^[0-9 ]*$'::text)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_email_partial_key ON auth.users USING btree (email) WHERE (is_sso_user = false)
CREATE INDEX users_instance_id_email_idx ON auth.users USING btree (instance_id, lower((email)::text))

tables.comment: Auth: Stores user login data within a secure schema.
